name: Garlic Allium sativum
summary: Garlic (Allium sativum) is most studied for cardiovascular support, including modest effects on blood pressure and lipids, alongside antimicrobial and immune-modulating activity from organosulfur compounds like allicin and ajoene.
description: Garlic (Allium sativum) is most studied for cardiovascular support, including modest effects on blood pressure and lipids, alongside antimicrobial and immune-modulating activity from organosulfur compounds like allicin and ajoene.
safetyNotes: GI upset; odor; potential bleeding risk at high intake

claimMap.claim: v9.6: Garlic/aged garlic extract has human evidence for modest blood-pressure support; bleeding cautions are important.
claimMap.notes: v9.6: Garlic/aged garlic extract has human evidence for modest blood-pressure support; bleeding cautions are important.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Aged garlic extract commonly 600–1200 mg/day in BP/lipid studies

dosage: Aged garlic extract (~1.2 mg S-allyl cysteine/day) or dried powder 600-1200 mg/day; composition varies by preparation
typical_dosage: Aged garlic extract (~1.2 mg S-allyl cysteine/day) or dried powder 600-1200 mg/day; composition varies by preparation
